danger_signals.visual_form: Box sprite positioned flush with wall tiles
danger_signals.meaning: Permanent trap—box cannot be retrieved if pushed here without exit path

danger_signals.visual_form: Box surrounded by walls on three sides with only one entry
danger_signals.meaning: High risk of deadlock if player enters and box blocks exit

danger_signals.visual_form: Two or more box sprites touching or in close proximity
danger_signals.meaning: Cannot push the inner box without moving outer box first; risk of cascading deadlock

danger_signals.visual_form: Player sprite surrounded by boxes with no empty floor squares to move to
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate loss state—movement impossible, puzzle unsolvable

safety_signals.visual_form: Unoccupied tile space with no box or wall
safety_signals.meaning: Safe movement available; player has options to reposition

safety_signals.visual_form: Designated spot tile (described as red dots in source) distinct from floor
safety_signals.meaning: Valid target position; box placed here solves that objective

safety_signals.visual_form: Multiple consecutive empty floor squares forming a path
safety_signals.meaning: Safe navigation route; allows player repositioning without box contact

safety_signals.visual_form: Box sprite overlapping or positioned on target marker
safety_signals.meaning: Partial goal achievement; one objective complete

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boxes not on all storage location markers; some red dot targets empty
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player retains full movement freedom; boxes respond to push input

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Box visually trapped (against wall, in corner, or blocked by other boxes) with no push vector available
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Puzzle becomes unsolvable regardless of future moves; player must undo steps to recover

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All boxes positioned on all storage location markers simultaneously
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Level completion triggered; progression to next level or level select available

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Box against wall or second box; no movement occurs
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Input rejected; player sprite does not advance; box does not move

environmental_storytelling.example: Warehouse layout with walls forming dead-ends and narrow corridors visually tells player 'planning required'—the physical environment enforces consequence for careless moves
Storage location markers (red dots) positioned in specific layout relative to boxes; their placement hints at intended solution path and spatial relationships
Some warehouse rooms are symmetrical (suggesting multiple solution paths) while others are asymmetrical (suggesting single optimal path); layout tells story of puzzle difficulty
Early levels show sparse box/target distribution; later levels pack more boxes into tighter spaces, visually communicating increasing difficulty

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Moves one square when player character moves into it from the opposite side; stops if target square is wall or another box
Remains stationary; may have visual indicator (color change, overlap with target sprite) showing completion
Cannot be pushed; becomes immobile deadlock indicator
Moves one square per input (horizontal or vertical) onto empty floor squares
Cannot move; input rejected if target square occupied by box or wall
Static; blocks both player and box movement; defines spatial boundary

notes: Super Sokoban (SNES, 1995) is a pure logic puzzle with minimal narrative framing. World-state communication relies entirely on spatial/visual arrangement of entities (player, boxes, walls, storage markers). No faction system, danger/safety signals are purely mechanical—derived from puzzle topology rather than aesthetic design. The warehouse setting provides minimal environmental storytelling; focus is on abstract puzzle state. Danger emerges from irreversible moves (deadlock) communicated through box position relative to walls/other boxes. Safety signals indicate reversibility and viable move options. Visual vocabulary is minimal: player sprite, box sprite, wall tile, floor tile, storage marker.
